The washerwomen of lincoln,
Neb., have organized a union and
adopted a scale of Is cents per
hour.
The o. R. & N. has let the con-
tract for a new 100000 depot at
Walla Walla to Robert Wakefield
Of Portland.


John McBirney has been elected
grand commander of the Idaho
knight Templars. The conclave
next June will be held at Occur
d'Alene.


After suffering for three weeks
with hiccoughs Albert Coldwell,
age SO. died at Olympia. He was
| taken to a hospital but treatment
| failed to relieve him and death en-
| sued from exhaustion.


John Berkstresser, pioneer
farmer of Garfield, has perfected
a hybrid corn that produces 40
bushels to the acre. The corn is
now ripe and ready to cut.


Roy Loucks, a barber, and a
woman said to be Mrs. Fl. J. Gillis-
pie. of iMinot, N. D., were arrested
on the statutory charge at Mos
COW, Idaho, yesterday, as they were
taking a train.


Jobbing interests on the Sound
and at Portland are combining to
fight Spokane's efforts to obtain
better freight rates. The West side
fears Spokane will secure the East
side trade, which the coast has
controlled in the past.


The Michigan Federation of
Labor, meeting today at Kalama-
ZOO, has under consideration re.
quest from the Detroit federation
to place William c. Mcmillan, can
didate for senator, on the spolitical
blacklist. If the action a. taken
an active labor campaign will be
made against him.





Suit has been started in Seattle
against o, E. Lindbloom, j; Under
berg and ]. Brynteson, di5eqverers
of Nome placer mines, to fedover
$1,666,6 $50 from each on alleged
misrepresentation of funds from the
Pioneer Mining company, j. e.
Chilberg, of Seattle, and several
other prominent Alaska mining
men are also named as defendants.


The big Weyerhaeuser mill at
Potlatch, known as the Potlatch
Lumber company is now in opera
tion. The plant has daily capacity
of 250, 900 feet of lumber and 250,-
900 Hath. II is claimed to be the
biggest lumber mill in the world.
The company has built town
around the plant and furnished it
with water and sewer systems.
